# Source Card: WHO Child Growth Standards: Weight-for-length/height

## Summary
This WHO Child Growth Standards portal page is the primary normative source defining the weight-for-length/height anthropometric indicator for children from birth to 5 years. It establishes the age-band split that a pediatric CDS must respect: weight-for-length (recumbent length) from birth to 2 years, and weight-for-height (standing height) from 2 to 5 years, joined into a combined birth-to-5-years standard. Reference distributions are published as both z-scores and percentiles, separately for girls and boys. The page is a distribution portal, so it defines the indicator's structure and age boundaries but does not itself publish the numeric classification cutoffs (wasting/overweight z-score thresholds) or the length-vs-height measurement adjustment.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Weight-for-length is the birth-to-2-years indicator — locator: 'Weight-for-length: Birth to 2 years' section label — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:0c8b284ddbb45298773f0f92a41e6a16020e65bf3e9b3d0d9e802b8a25fbc918]"
- (ev_002) Weight-for-height is the 2-to-5-years indicator — locator: 'Weight-for-height: 2 to 5 years' section label — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:07d3ed3e06941c3735bb3b18fb2a4312174566e8af18d03af5d8e05455e358f8]"
- (ev_003) Combined standard spans birth to 5 years — locator: combined 'Birth to 5 years' label — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:4e7284951072ddc0e62a27a2ecab8b61ceee4fde291a640ff6fcb9e59adc7ea3]"
- (ev_004) Published as z-scores and percentiles, separately by sex — locator: Charts/Tables subsection labels — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:0018d15f4312b2659068b22305d1d4e2c2c43f99c98b39f18117d73d9389c756]"
- (ev_005) Multiple derived publication formats — locator: resource-category headings — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:5e1559c9c8a5e9be41938f2c16b719da78653e9689cad23a62dd2589b3d45de3]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population: birth to 5 years, both sexes, published as sex-specific distributions. The measurement basis changes at the 2-year boundary: recumbent length (weight-for-length, birth-2y) vs standing height (weight-for-height, 2-5y). A CDS converter MUST branch on age at 24 months to pick the correct indicator/table.
- Assay/method: pure anthropometry (length/height + weight); not laboratory-assay dependent, so no analyzer/method harmonization concerns. Measurement-technique fidelity (recumbent vs standing) is the analog of assay dependence here.
- Lifecycle: WHO Child Growth Standards released 2006; supersede the older 1977 NCHS/WHO growth reference within the WHO program. No retirement date; still the current WHO standard as of access date.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- This page does not define a red-flag or referral trigger by itself. The clinically actionable scope-exit thresholds (e.g., weight-for-length/height z-score < -2 = wasting, < -3 = severe wasting; > +2/+3 = overweight/obese) are the classification cutoffs applied ON TOP of this reference distribution and are NOT published on this page (see Limitations). Any referral trigger in the CDS must cite those cutoffs from their own primary source, not this portal page.

## Limitations & conflicts
- GAP — z-score classification cutoffs not locatable in source: the wasting (< -2 SD), severe wasting (< -3 SD), and overweight/obese (> +2 / +3 SD) thresholds are NOT stated on this distribution page; they must be sourced verbatim from the WHO growth-standards methodology report / WHO-AnthroPlus or the WHO/UNICEF wasting definitions before any CDS classification rule asserts them.
- GAP — length-vs-height measurement adjustment not locatable in source: the commonly cited ~0.7 cm recumbent-length-minus-standing-height correction applied around the 2-year boundary is NOT quoted on this page; do not assert that number from this source. Source it from the WHO measurement/training module if the CDS needs it.
- GAP — exact publication month/edition string not locatable on the portal page (year 2006 taken from the WHO Child Growth Standards program; page does not print a full version string).
- Scope limit: this is a distribution portal, not a methodology document; treat structural labels (age bands, sex split, z-score/percentile forms) as the citable facts and defer numeric clinical cutoffs to the underlying WHO technical report.
- No direct conflicts recorded with other assigned sources; note that WHO weight-for-length/height (0-5y) is a distinct indicator from BMI-for-age (used ≥2y in some schemes) — keep them separate in the CDS, do not collapse.
